summary: PR #2442 linked several item translation units by moving six item source files from NonMatching to Matching, adjusting item source/header code, and updating GALE01 symbol metadata for local functions, local literals, section labels, and data object sizes. Bot report evidence shows +21216 linked-code bytes, +5984 matched-code bytes, 19 new matches, and 5 unmatched-item improvements.

key_files.role: Changed ittaru, itfoxblaster, ityoshieggthrow, itclimbersstring, itclinkmilk, and itkirby_2F23 from NonMatching to Matching.
Updated function/data symbol metadata, including local scope changes, local @NNN literal symbols, section labels, and data object sizes.
Reworked Fox blaster item vars from repeated scalar groups into arrays for xDE4, xDFC, xE14, and angle.
Updated Fox blaster code to use array fields and a reverse shift loop instead of manually repeated scalar assignments.
Largest item source rewrite in the slice; includes include cleanup, inline helper movement/addition, PAD_STACK adjustments, and matched item function/data progress.
Item source linked as Matching; bot report lists .data and .sdata2 reaching 100% and two functions reaching 100%.
Item source linked as Matching; includes PAD_STACK adjustment and bot-reported .data/function matches.
Item source linked as Matching; bot report lists .data plus function matches.
Item source linked as Matching; bot report lists two functions reaching 100%.
Item source improved; bot report lists one function and .sdata2 reaching 100%, plus one unmatched-item improvement.
Item source improved but not linked; bot report lists it_80289BE8 improvement.
Item source improved but not linked; bot report lists two unmatched-item improvements and PAD_STACK adjustment in diff.
Item source improved but not linked; bot report lists one new match and one unmatched-item improvement, with PAD_STACK adjustment in diff.

what_changed: Six item TUs were switched to Matching in configure.py: ittaru.c, itfoxblaster.c, ityoshieggthrow.c, itclimbersstring.c, itclinkmilk.c, and itkirby_2F23.c.
The Fox blaster item variable layout in itCharItems.h changed repeated scalar fields into arrays: xDE4[6], xDFC[6], xE14[6], and angle[6].
itfoxblaster.c replaced a repeated manual scalar shift block with a for loop over those arrays and removed a comment describing the earlier non-loop form as likely fake improvement.

symbols.txt changed many item data/string/float symbols from global address-style names to local @NNN symbols and added local data labels such as ...data.0; several object sizes/scopes were also adjusted.
Bot report recorded 19 new matches, including complete data matches for ittaru, itclinkmilk, itkirby_2F23, and several individual function matches across item TUs.
Bot report recorded 5 improvements in still-unmatched items: itkusudama, itkyasarin, itpikachuthunder, and itwstar.

smart_moves.text: Turning the Fox blaster repeated field groups into arrays enabled ordinary indexed code and a reverse shift loop, aligning with the natural-loops standard instead of preserving manually repeated generated-looking assignments.
The PR paired source changes with symbol metadata changes, including localizing TU-owned functions/literals and splitting data objects, rather than compensating only in C source.
The configure.py linking change was backed by an automated report showing linked-code and matched-code gains plus specific new matches.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When several adjacent item fields form parallel repeated groups, consider whether the recovered struct should use arrays; once typed as arrays, shift/update code can often become a natural loop.
A TU-linking PR may require symbol metadata cleanup for local strings, floats, rodata, sdata, sdata2, and object sizes in addition to C source edits.
PAD_STACK changes appeared as narrow matching tactics in multiple item files; the PR evidence supports treating them as per-function codegen tactics, not as broad style.
evidence_refs: raw/diff.diff grep hits for PAD_STACK in itclimbersstring.c, itclinkmilk.c, itkyasarin.c, itpikachuthunder.c

naming_conventions.convention: Recovered array fields kept the existing offset-style names with array syntax, e.g. xDE4[6], xDFC[6], xE14[6], and angle[6], rather than inventing new semantic names for every slot.
Local TU-owned literals in symbols.txt were represented as local @NNN labels instead of global it_80xxxxxx names where this PR adjusted ownership/scope.
Unproven functions largely retained address-style or existing names such as it_802ADF10 and it_802C248C; the evidence does not show broad semantic renaming.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use array field recovery plus a reverse for loop to match repeated state-history shifting in itfoxblaster.c.
Adjust PAD_STACK amounts narrowly in affected functions, including observed changes such as 16 to 12 in itclinkmilk.c, 40 to 32 in itkyasarin.c, and 4 to 12 in itpikachuthunder.c.
Move or introduce static inline helpers in item source files to control codegen and readability, visible in itclimbersstring.c and other changed item files.
evidence_refs: raw/diff.diff grep hits for static inline

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Update symbol scope and sizes for local data/literals as part of TU linking rather than relying only on C source changes.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Automated GALE01 report showed matched code 65.40% (+0.15%, +5984 bytes), linked code 37.87% (+0.55%, +21216 bytes), matched data 38.67% (+0.07%, +864 bytes), linked data 32.98% (+0.09%, +1120 bytes), 19 new matches, and 5 unmatched-item improvements.
No human review comments or review records were included in the loaded PR slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Parallel scalar item fields may indicate array fields and natural loops
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2442, Fox blaster item vars changed from repeated scalar groups to arrays and the source replaced a manual repeated shift block with a reverse for loop. This is a good graph candidate for item-var recovery cases where adjacent fields form repeated parallel slots.

accepted_candidate_records.title: TU linking can require symbol metadata ownership cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2442 linked several item TUs while also changing symbols.txt scopes, @NNN local literals, local section labels, and data object sizes. This supports a narrow candidate that item TU linking may require section/symbol metadata work when the source match exposes local ownership.

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2442 linked six item source files as Matching
source_update_candidates.text: configure.py changed ittaru.c, itfoxblaster.c, ityoshieggthrow.c, itclimbersstring.c, itclinkmilk.c, and itkirby_2F23.c from NonMatching to Matching.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a path/configuration fact from the PR diff, but should be curated against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Fox blaster item vars use arrays after PR #2442
source_update_candidates.text: itCharItems.h changed Fox blaster variable groups to xDE4[6], xDFC[6], xE14[6], and angle[6].
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful recovered-layout fact, but source-owned header facts must be verified against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2442 localized many item literals and floats in symbols.txt
source_update_candidates.text: symbols.txt changed numerous item string/float/data symbols from global it_80xxxxxx names to local @NNN labels and adjusted local section labels/object sizes.
source_update_candidates.reason: Symbol metadata changes are source-owned path facts; the PR evidence supports a candidate but not promotion without current-symbol verification.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every PAD_STACK use in these files is desirable style; the PR only supports narrow matching-tactic use in specific functions.
Do not infer human reviewer rationale; no human review comments were included.
Do not promote the bot report as proof of current repository state; it is historical PR evidence.
Do not generalize local @NNN symbols as a naming preference for all literals; in this PR they are metadata changes tied to TU/data ownership.
